steps.title: Identify the sides relative to angle A
steps.content:
In a right triangle, tan(angle) = opposite/adjacent.

For angle A, the opposite side is BC and the adjacent side is AC. From the labeled side lengths on the figure, the side opposite angle A measures $2\sqrt{3}$ and the side adjacent to angle A measures $2$.
$$\tan A = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{2\sqrt{3}}{2}$$

steps.title: Simplify the ratio
steps.content:
Cancel the common factor of $2$ in the numerator and denominator:
$$\frac{2\sqrt{3}}{2} = \sqrt{3}$$
This is the tangent of a $60^\circ$ angle, consistent with the $30$-$60$-$90$ ratio (opposite: adjacent: hypotenuse $= \sqrt{3}k: k: 2k$). That matches choice C — C is correct.
